summary: Added a deny-by-default evaluator that separates snapshot consistency and reconciliation validity from canonical queue mutation authority.
observed: Authority is disabled unless an explicit enabled policy allows the exact actor, workflow, canonical path, operation, source commit, source digest, and candidate digest.
Requests expire and carry a digest over their stable contents.
Rejected files, orphan projections, unresolved queue items, prior mutation, and candidate self-authorization all deny materialization.
The evaluator returns a decision but never performs mutation.
inferred: This closes the previously recorded semantic gap between a consistent reconciliation candidate and authorization to replace canonical queue state.
proposed: Add a separately reviewed mutation executor that consumes only an authorized decision and exact candidate bytes, then records the resulting commit.
superseded: A valid snapshot-native reconciliation candidate is sufficient authority to update operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
unresolved: The committed tests were not executed by an observed runtime in this run.
No cryptographic signer or GitHub environment protection is yet bound to the actor and workflow strings.
No mutation executor exists, by design.
privacy_boundary: Public repository state and non-personal operational metadata only. Raw chat, health data, credentials, identifying personal information, and unreviewed file-library material were excluded.
verification_route: Run node --test tools/continuity/materialization-authority.test.mjs in CI, then bind policy identity to a protected GitHub environment or signed attestation before implementing a mutation executor.
